reviews.review_title: Lots of fun and great workout
reviews.review_description: Added to my daily workout list.

While I picked this up with minimal expections, I can see it becoming one of my favorite apps and a regular part of my VR fitness routine.  The game is all about running large obstacle courses and includes a cute but easily ignored story line related to titular Mr. Venture.  The game starts with a tutorial which does a great job allowing you to get comfortable with the basic controls.  I have only played the first obstacle course (twice now), but I have found the fun factor to be so high that I feel compelled to write this review now - the game is already 5 stars for me.  The first course is really huge and includes a great variety of interesting mechanics (crouching, climbing, running, timing sections, rotating handles, simple puzzles).  Fortunately there are multiple checkpoints so it never gets frustrating and I can see replaying this level until I get a perfect run.  While the graphics are just decent, they get the job done.  The controls are well thought out and the physics seem good although I did find the continued momentum when you stop running to be a bit frustrating at times.  I also feel like I am getting a good workout while really enjoying myself.  

The only games comparable to Venture’s Gauntlet that I have played in VR is the excellent Climb series.  This game deserves to find it’s audience and I highly recommend it.

Highly terrifying, quite original in it's presentation and style. I enjoy it more than I thought I would. Become a contestant in the "Gauntlet", a vertigo inducing parkour death maze with the top prize being the inheritance of Billionaire Psychopath Adam Venture. It's not one for those without strong V.R legs and will totally mess with your fear of heights (if you have one) in a somehow  very entertaining way.  Hardcore, gruelling, eccentric, quirky and atmospheric. Like a cross between "Mirror's Edge" and a recurring childhood nightmare.  The graphics are simple but have a certain aesthetic to them that really fits with the game. The music is pretty good too. The control system is responsive and fluid. Obviously it's rock hard, but the only real complaint I have is about the terrible , janky menu system. I also would have liked to been told that there is no mid-save stage. I understand why, I just would have liked to have been informed. Apart from that It's a very engaging experience in the main. BTW the reviewer that stated the movement was snail-like obviously didn't listen to instructions: You have to move your arms in a jogging motion to move at speed. Forward on the joystick is just for fine control.

It's a unique VR experience.  Kinda like what stunt runner could have been in rec room but more physical.  I loved the climbing and dodging.  There's definitely some jank in the movement they need to fix, especially when trying to block shots with you shield.  Very annoying at certain sections but I powered through and still enjoyed the game.

Swing your arms to run, not like Gorilla Tag, but more like in real life. It does feel quite natural and comfortable, except there's no way to run backwards (to correct running too far), except using your joystick which moves very slowly. Aside from that drawback, it's very fun. There's climbing, dodging/hiding, crouching, etc. Graphics is okay.
Real Great game and fun for all  see how far you can get. keep up the great work guys .hope to see more things to come .
really clever use of natural mechanics! Makes you forget all the workout you are making while you embark in such a good adventure

I played the training mission twice, and both the times , there is a part where you have to hold a wooden stick on a rope with both hands that transports you a few meters farther, but when I land on the wooden platform, it seems that my legs are too long in the game, and I drop/fall /sink below the wooden floor where I landed, becoming entrapped surrounded by 3 wooden walls. I have to exit to the game menu. I guess this can be fixed easy. Anyhow, the training mission parts that I played are fun. The game looks good and there is some nice humor. Got it for cheap, so here is 5 stars for the team.

*Full video review over on my YouTube channel*

Ventures Gauntlet has come over from the PC to the Quest and its exellent.

A very hard VR Obstacle course over 9 levels.

Jumped in with Clickster and we had so much fun. The game mechanics of running with your arms swinging, climbing walls, ropes, dodging lasers there is everything and the levels are quite long also.

Multiplayer is coming but what there is now is a huge workout and i was sweating by the end of it.

Totally worth it if you are looking for a new workout title with a twist.

I first experienced VG on pcvr when it launched on Steam last year. Immediately found it incredibly fun and engaging. It's a obstacle course on steroids! Running (by pumping your arms), climbing across bars, ropes and platforms, with the added danger of heights, avoiding lasers, dodging being shot at by drones and being able to shoot back at times. There is a back story that drives you to work to improve your times across the various segments, each progressively more difficult.
If this kind of challenging, somewhat physical, gamey experience appeals to you, you really should try Ventures Gauntlet.
But don't think for a minute you'll win Ventures fortune. That's all mine! 😆

reviews.review_title: Quite good. Tired after one level :p
reviews.review_description:
The concept is simple but perfect.

One level a day is a good warm up.

I'm a big fan of VR climbing, parkour, etc (eg Stride, No More Rainbows, Battle Talent) and I'm enjoying this game. I'll be replaying levels to improve my time like I did with Stride or The Climb.

The maps are huge and they made the effort to include voice actors. They lean a bit on the Portal style, which I didnt like in the beginning. But it has some charm and decent sense of humour.

Now some aspects of the game are a bit rough and can be improved, but this is a solid start and I hope the game gets the traction it deserves.
This is a fun way to break a nice sweat!
It's a fun game. Get it.
This game has a great story, as well as smooth & intuitive movement and workout mechanics. Absolutely loving it so far

developer_response.body: Thank you so much!  We hope you survive the gauntlet 😁💪

reviews.review_description: Quick way to burn calories , and unique fun game .

I like the idea of this app and I hope some updates make it more enjoyable. The arm swing movement style works great but there is no option for smooth turning, I personally hate snap turn and it really lowers my enjoyment of this game. I also think a jump button (combined with arm swing like battle talent) would be much appreciated. If your going to be running and swinging it makes sense that you would jump also. Maybe the ability to swim which could make the levels more dynamic and provide additional ways to get to the exit. Overall this is a pretty decent game for parkor and VR exercise but it needs a little bit of tweeking to be great.

developer_response.body:
Hi Kevin thank you for the feedback, we will implement smooth turn on our next update a lot of users have been asking for that.  

Swimming would be cool, we have some ideas for some new maps which are a little less uni-directional so that would be fun.

reviews.review_description: Platforming in VR (where you play as a human being) finally !! Been waiting for a game like this for ages. Very fun and a decent workout (especially if you're going for speed). A little rough around the edges and the movement takes a little getting used to, but still great fun and a gem that I highly recommend.

Game needs a major patch to fix movement issues - and these issues are prevalent and annoying. 

Walking is basically useless. You move nowhere and I'm sure a snail has more speed. If you want to be careful with movement, then walking needs to be a decent rate. 

Grabbing is very broken with the grip not working many times and then when you try to pull yourself up from a ledge it becomes worse. Every single time, trying to pull up either doesn't work, or launches me somewhere else. 

Also there's still no smooth turning, are you kidding?! VR is supposed to be more advanced and you continue to omit the most basic of turn styles - and make people use click turning? 

Also, the price was $11.99 CAD for so long, then all of a sudden it jumped to $19.99, yet there's still issues. 
What kind of scam is that? 

This is fun, sometimes, but until there's a fix you should save your money.

reviews.review_title: Cool climbing but a few things are bad
reviews.review_description:
The climbing sections and swing parts are very good but the movment and bow is just bad sometimes when you dont want to walk but need to reach a button you walk or duck on a moving platform (i think it was level 3) you just walk of the platform and when you need to get a arrow into the bow fast it just wont and it isnt so accurate  and that makes some levels unfair like the bow section in level 6 
That makes the game often unfun but apart from the bow and walking its a good game but sadly because of the flaws i give it 2 stars
